perturbation.original_text: "Interest Rate" means (a) with respect to a non-defaulting Party, a per annum rate of interest equal to USD 1M LIBOR as per the last fixing of the preceding month + 3.5 per cent pro anno (compounded monthly for each month or part thereof) and (b) with respect to a Defaulting Party, a per annum rate of interest equal to 5 % over the rate mentioned above in (a), provided, how- ever, that the rate set forth in (a) shall be adjusted by the Parties annually at the last meeting of the JDMB (as such term is defined in the Heads of Agreement entered into between the Parties as of January 10, 2020.
perturbation.changed_text: "Interest Rate" means (a) with respect to a non-defaulting Party, a per annum rate of interest to be determined at the discretion of Bunker One and (b) with respect to a Defaulting Party, a per annum rate of interest equal to 5 % over the rate mentioned above in (a), provided, how- ever, that the rate set forth in (a) shall be adjusted by the Parties annually at the last meeting of the JDMB (as such term is defined in the Heads of Agreement entered into between the Parties as of January 10, 2020.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Interest Rate' to give Bunker One discretion in determining the rate for non-defaulting parties, it creates a contradiction when calculating interest on disputed ledger statements (Section 4.5). The original text provided a specific formula based on USD 1M LIBOR + 3.5%, while the modified text gives Bunker One unilateral control, creating an in-text contradiction in how disputes are resolved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment for any amounts due in accordance with the above shall be made within three (3) Business Days after the date of the applicable detailed ledger Statement. If any detailed ledger State- ment is disputed in good faith, the Party owing money will pay the undisputed portion by the due date and will by such due date provide a written explanation of the basis for the disputed portion to such due date. The Parties shall endeavor to resolve any disputes promptly, and the disputed amount found due, if any, plus accrued interest at the Interest Rate shall be paid within three (3) Business Days following resolution of the dispute. All payments under the terms of this Agreement shall be made in US Dollars and immediately available funds. The Parties shall net all undisputed amounts due and owing on the same day, and/or past due, arising under this Agreement such that the Party owing the greater amount shall make a single payment of the net amount to the other Party. Interest on late payments shall accrue at the Interest Rate from the due date until the date of payment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a defined interest rate (USD 1M LIBOR + 3.5%) for non-defaulting parties with a rate determined at the discretion of Bunker One. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies that interest on disputed amounts will accrue 'at the Interest Rate'. Since the interest rate is no longer fixed by a formula but rather at Bunker One's discretion, it creates a strong in-text contradiction because there is no longer a consistent method to calculate accrued interest, causing uncertainty regarding the interest calculation.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3. Bunker One shall prepare and provide to Vertex, on a monthly and quarterly basis, a statement setting forth the status of the detailed ledger and each component thereof (the "detailed ledger Statement"). The detailed ledger Statement and all other reports and settlement statements provided for herein shall be in such format as may be agreed upon by the Parties from time to time and shall include such supporting documentation as is reasonably necessary or reasonably requested by Vertex to enable Vertex to verify the accuracy of such report or settlement statement. Bunker One shall prepare and maintain such other accounting and transaction records as may be necessary to provide a full accounting of all transactions and other activities under this Agree- ment. Vertex has the right, at its sole expense and during normal working hours, to have a third party accountant examine the records of Bunker One. If any such examination reveals any inaccuracy in any statement, the necessary adjustments in such statement and the payments thereof will be made promptly and shall bear interest calculated at the Interest Rate from the date the overpayment or underpayment was made until paid.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3. Bunker One shall prepare and provide to Vertex, on a monthly and quarterly basis, a statement setting forth the status of the detailed ledger and each component thereof (the "detailed ledger Statement"). The detailed ledger Statement and all other reports and settlement statements provided for herein shall be in such format as determined by Bunker One. Bunker One shall prepare and maintain such other accounting and transaction records as may be necessary to provide a full accounting of all transactions and other activities under this Agree- ment. Vertex has the right, at its sole expense and during normal working hours, to have a third party accountant examine the records of Bunker One. If any such examination reveals any inaccuracy in any statement, the necessary adjustments in such statement and the payments thereof will be made promptly and shall bear interest calculated at the Interest Rate from the date the overpayment or underpayment was made until paid.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause in Section 3.3 requiring 'supporting documentation as is reasonably necessary or reasonably requested by Vertex to enable Vertex to verify the accuracy of such report or settlement statement', it creates a contradiction in the rights afforded to Vertex. Previously, Vertex had the right to receive sufficient documentation to verify the statements; now, with that clause omitted and the 'format' being 'determined by Bunker One' it diminishes Vertex's ability to effectively exercise its right to examine Bunker One's records via a third-party accountant as stated later in that same paragraph. Therefore, this alteration creates an internal contradiction and diminishes Vertexes Power.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Bunker One shall prepare and provide to Vertex, on a monthly and quarterly basis, a statement setting forth the status of the detailed ledger and each component thereof (the "detailed ledger Statement"). The detailed ledger Statement and all other reports and settlement statements provided for herein shall be in such format as determined by Bunker One. Bunker One shall prepare and maintain such other accounting and transaction records as may be necessary to provide a full accounting of all transactions and other activities under this Agree- ment. Vertex has the right, at its sole expense and during normal working hours, to have a third party accountant examine the records of Bunker One. If any such examination reveals any inaccuracy in any statement, the necessary adjustments in such statement and the payments thereof will be made promptly and shall bear interest calculated at the Interest Rate from the date the overpayment or underpayment was made until paid.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3.3 required Bunker One to provide Vertex with supporting documentation necessary for verifying the accuracy of the reports. The changed text removes this requirement and gives Bunker One the sole discretion to determine the format of the statements. This directly contradicts Vertex's right to examine Bunker One's records through a third-party accountant, because without supporting documentation, Vertex's ability to verify the accuracy of the statements is significantly diminished. Thus it is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All fees incurred, and profit/loss incurred in connection with hedging of the JSMA Output shall be for the account of the JSMA. All hedging is to be done on a physical, not specula- tive, basis.
perturbation.changed_text: All fees incurred, and profit/loss incurred in connection with hedging of the JSMA Output shall be for the account of the JSMA.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'All hedging is to be done on a physical, not speculative, basis' in Section 2.5, it now contradicts the intent described in recital D, where Bunker One was responsible for 'the risk management/exposure (e.g. hedging) of the bunker fuels' in a way that suggests practical management, not speculation. The omission now allows for speculative hedging, conflicting with the earlier stated objective that highlighted the company's responsibility towards handling the bunkers carefully.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The overall objective of the Original COOA was to give the Parties the opportunity to further expand their business by co-operating in the sourcing, storing, transportation, marketing and selling of the Product in and around Louisiana and Texas, where (i) Vertex was primarily responsible for the sourcing and storing of the feedstock Product, (ii) Bunker One was primarily responsible for the transporting, blending, marketing, selling and delivering of the Product, (iii) Bunker One was responsible for the risk management/exposure (e.g. hedging) of the bunker fuels, and (iv) Bunker One was the exclusive seller of the Product to third parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that all hedging was to be done on a physical, not speculative, basis. The changed text removes this restriction, allowing for speculative hedging. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that Bunker One was responsible for the 'risk management/exposure (e.g. hedging) of the bunker fuels,' implying a careful, practical approach to risk management, not speculative activities. Removing the explicit prohibition on speculation introduces ambiguity and contradicts the previously understood intent of Bunker One's responsibilities as described earlier in the contract.
